notes: Reported additional benchmarks: SWE-Bench Pro (public) 57.7%, GDPval 83.0%, OSWorld-Verified 75.0%, Toolathlon 54.6%, BrowseComp 82.7%, MMMU Pro (no tools) 81.2%, Tau2-bench Telecom (none) 64.3%. API pricing update notes: Batch and Flex are available at 50% of standard rates, Priority processing is 2x standard rates.

Artificial Analysis reports AAIndex 48, 204 tok/s, total cost to run the Intelligence Index $1353.82, and 240M output tokens used. OpenAI reports 400k context, image input support, tool use, and additional benchmarks including OSWorld-Verified 72.1%, Toolathlon 42.9%, HLE w/ tools 41.5%, and MMMUPro 76.6%.

Artificial Analysis reports AAIndex 44, 192.5 tok/s, total cost to run the Intelligence Index $375.99, and 210M output tokens used. OpenAI positions nano as the lowest-cost GPT-5.4 variant for high-volume API workloads and reports additional benchmarks including Toolathlon 35.5%, HLE w/ tools 37.7%, and OSWorld-Verified 39.0%.
Cerebras reports provider speed of ~3000 tokens/s for gpt-oss-120b. Other benchmark and pricing values may still reflect cross-provider or non-Cerebras sources.

No-caching pricing: $0.25 input / $1.50 output per 1M tokens. Reported additional benchmarks: MMMU-Pro 76.8%, CharXiv Reasoning 73.2%, Video-MMMU 84.8%, SimpleQA Verified 43.3%, FACTS Benchmark Suite 40.6%, MMMLU 88.9%, LiveCodeBench 72.0%, MRCR v2 (128k avg) 60.1%, MRCR v2 (1M pointwise) 12.3%, output speed 363 tok/s.

Artificial Analysis lists Gemma 4 31B (Reasoning) as an open-weights model from Google with text, image, and video input, text output, a 256k context window, 30.7B total parameters, Apache 2.0 license, and Hugging Face weights. Public benchmarks on the model page include GDPval-AA 31%, Terminal-Bench Hard 36%, tau^2-Bench Telecom 66%, AA-LCR 62%, AA-Omniscience Accuracy 20%, AA-Omniscience Non-Hallucination Rate 18%, IFBench 76%, CritPt 1%, SciCode 43%, and MMMU-Pro 75%. The FAQ also reports 1.61s TTFT based on Google's API.
Artificial Analysis reports 9.01s time to first token, text and image input support, text output, and proprietary status. The Artificial Analysis model page lists March 10, 2026 as the release date.

Artificial Analysis reports 155.3 tokens/s, 0.90s TTFT, non-reasoning behavior, 128k context, and pricing of $0.10 / $0.10 per 1M tokens. Cerebras reports a provider-specific speed of ~2200 tokens/s for this model.

Meta describes Muse Spark as the first Muse-series model, powering Meta AI across the app and website with multimodal reasoning, and rolling out more broadly across Meta products with private-preview API access for select partners. Artificial Analysis lists Muse Spark as a proprietary reasoning model with text, image, and speech input, text output, and a 262k context window. Public benchmarks on the model page include GDPval-AA 47%, Terminal-Bench Hard 46%, tau^2-Bench Telecom 92%, AA-LCR 70%, AA-Omniscience Accuracy 45%, AA-Omniscience Non-Hallucination Rate 27%, IFBench 76%, CritPt 11%, MMMU-Pro 81%, and SciCode 52%. Pricing and output speed were shown as unavailable on the referenced page, so those fields are intentionally omitted here.

Artificial Analysis reports 423.9 tokens/s, 0.70s time-to-first-token, and a blended price of $0.41 per 1M tokens. NVIDIA reports Nemotron 3 Super scores 85.6% on PinchBench and uses a 120B total / 12B active-parameter hybrid Mamba-Transformer latent MoE architecture.

Artificial Analysis lists GLM-5.1 (Reasoning) as an open-weights text-only model from Z AI with a 200k context window, 754B total parameters, MIT license, and Hugging Face weights. Public benchmarks on the model page include GDPval-AA 52%, Terminal-Bench Hard 42%, tau^2-Bench Telecom 98%, AA-LCR 62%, AA-Omniscience Accuracy 24%, AA-Omniscience Non-Hallucination Rate 71%, IFBench 76%, CritPt 5%, and SciCode 44%. Output speed, pricing, and TTFT on the page are described as medians across providers serving the model.

Artificial Analysis reports GLM-4.7 (Reasoning) at 94.4 tokens/s, 1.25s TTFT, 200k context, $0.60 / $2.20 per 1M tokens, 170M output tokens, and 357B total / 32B active parameters. Z.ai launched GLM-4.7 on 2025-12-22 and reports gains over GLM-4.6 across coding, tool use, and reasoning, including SWE-bench Verified 73.8%, SWE-bench Multilingual 66.7%, Terminal Bench 2.0 41.0%, tau^2-Bench 87.4%, and HLE w/ Tools 42.8%. Cerebras separately reports a provider-specific speed of ~1000 tokens/s for this model.

Reported additional benchmarks: TAU2-Bench 86.7, BFCL-V4 72.9, BrowseComp 69.0/78.6, MMMU-Pro 79.0, OmniDocBench1.5 90.8, OSWorld-Verified 62.2. Qwen3.5-Plus (hosted) reports a 1M context window by default.

Artificial Analysis reports this non-reasoning text model has 235B total parameters with 22B active, uses the Apache 2.0 license, and has 2.46s TTFT based on Alibaba's API. Artificial Analysis reports 55.1 tokens/s, while Cerebras reports a provider-specific speed of ~1400 tokens/s for this model.

Artificial Analysis lists Qwen3.6 Plus as a proprietary reasoning model from Alibaba with text, image, and video input, text output, and a 1M context window. Public benchmarks on the model page include GDPval-AA 44%, Terminal-Bench Hard 44%, tau^2-Bench Telecom 98%, AA-LCR 70%, AA-Omniscience Accuracy 24%, AA-Omniscience Non-Hallucination Rate 68%, IFBench 75%, CritPt 3%, SciCode 41%, and MMMU-Pro 78%. The page also reports 2.29s TTFT based on Alibaba's API.

Official MiniMax launch post says M2.7 is available on MiniMax Agent and the MiniMax API Platform, with native agent-team workflows and dynamic tool search. Reported benchmarks include SWE-Pro 56.22%, VIBE-Pro 55.6%, Terminal Bench 2 57.0%, GDPval-AA 1495 Elo, Toolathon 46.3%, and MM Claw 62.7%, plus a 97% skill adherence rate across 40 complex skills. Artificial Analysis reports this reasoning model is text-only, proprietary, and available via two API providers; its model page lists a 205k context window and 3.31s TTFT, while the FAQ rounds context to 200k and blended pricing to $0.53 per 1M tokens.
Artificial Analysis page for the reasoning variant reports text-only input/output, 685B total parameters with 37B active, MIT license, and 1.95s TTFT based on DeepSeek's API.

Xiaomi positions MiMo-V2-Pro as its flagship agent model with over 1T total parameters, 42B active parameters, text-only IO, and tiered API pricing of $1/$3 up to 256k and $2/$6 from 256k to 1M tokens. Artificial Analysis reports AAIndex 49, 77M output tokens used for the Intelligence Index, and $350.87 total eval cost. OpenRouter reports 1,048,576 total context, 131.1k max output tokens, and provider throughput around 41 tok/s on Xiaomi. Xiaomi also reports ClawEval 81.0, PinchBench 1426, GDPVal-AA 96.8, tau2-bench Telecom 61.5, SWE-bench Verified 78.0, SWE-bench Multilingual 71.7, Terminal-Bench 2.0 57.1, and DeepSearch QA-F1 86.7.
Artificial Analysis page reports text-only input/output, 309B total parameters with 15B active, MIT license, and 2.29s TTFT based on the median across providers.
